Why local market context matters, a simple moving-box system, and a nearby farmers market.
Gwinnett & Beyond
A short weekly update for homeowners, friends, and neighbors across Gwinnett and the surrounding counties.
Wednesday, July 29, 2026

Hey friends,

We often talk about “the market” as though every neighborhood and county moves together. In reality, conditions can change quite a bit across a short drive.

That is one reason good real estate advice should be local and specific to the home, price range, and community involved.

This week’s quick comparison shows what I mean — and the homeowner tip may save you from opening twelve boxes to find one coffee maker.
MARKET SNAPSHOT
Nearby counties are not moving identically

In May 2026, active listings were up about 11.2% year over year in Gwinnett County. But nearby Barrow County was up about 50.3%, while Jackson County was up about 20.9%, according to Realtor.com.

Those differences matter. More listings can create more choices for buyers, but the effect on an individual home still depends on location, condition, price range, and nearby competition.

The lesson is not to make a decision from a national headline. Start with the numbers closest to the property you care about.

Pre-send check: Source: Realtor.com Economic Research county inventory data for May 2026, updated June 3, 2026.
HOMEOWNER TIP OF THE WEEK
Give every moving box a destination.

Whether you are moving soon or just organizing storage, label each box with three things:

  • The room where it belongs
  • A short list of what is inside
  • A simple priority such as Open First, This Week, or Storage

It takes a few extra seconds while packing and saves a surprising amount of time later. Keep one separate essentials box with medications, chargers, basic tools, and the coffee supplies.
AROUND GWINNETT: LOCAL EVENT OF THE WEEK

For a nearby weekend outing, here is another Gwinnett market worth checking.

Snellville Farmers Market

The Snellville Farmers Market is a community stop for seasonal produce, local foods, and small-business vendors near the Towne Green area.

Tip: Check the City of Snellville’s official events page for the current date, hours, location, vendors, and weather information.
CARTOON CORNER
“Good news: we labeled every box except the one we need.”

Moving has a remarkable sense of humor.
Know someone who may need real estate help?
